original_abstract: This volume brings together a collection of essays by distinguished historians from Austria, Germany, Ukraine, Poland, and Russia, offering a rigorous examination of a frequently overlooked dimension of the First World War: the 1918 occupation of Ukraine by the Central Powers. By delving into archives and diverse national perspectives, the contributors provide a nuanced new outlook on the complex historical landscape surrounding Ukraine’s fight for independence in the wake of the Great War.
humanized_abstract: This book constitutes the proceedings of the international historical conference "Deutsche, österreichische und tsaristische Oriente-Visionen auf dem Boden der Ukrainischen Republik 1918" held at Zharky in Ukraine in September 2017. The volume publishes the papers of well-known historians from Austria, Germany, Ukraine, Poland and Russia dealing with the generally neglected theme of the Central Powers? occupation of Ukraine in 1918. Drawing on the variety of sources and perspectives at their disposal the authors contribute to a new understanding of the volatile historical situation in Ukraine in 1918, a period of struggle for the country's independence in the aftermath of the "Great War".
